steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank names a quality of the research that makes the museum displays notable. The dash introduces the evidence: "the museum staff consulted numerous sources to ensure the accuracy of the displays." The correct word must describe careful, thorough attention to getting things right.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rigor': Rigor means exactness and careful, disciplined attention to detail. Consulting numerous sources to ensure accuracy is the definition of rigorous research. Fits precisely. ✓
  • Choice B — 'obscurity': Obscurity means being little known or unclear. Nothing in the passage suggests the research is hidden or unknown — the whole sentence praises it. Contradicts the positive framing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'shallowness': Shallowness is the direct opposite of consulting numerous sources to ensure accuracy. The passage explicitly says the research was thorough. ✗
  • Choice D — 'novelty': Novelty means newness or originality. The passage gives no indication that the research was innovative or unprecedented — only that it was careful and accurate. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
